title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
problem_statement: Remove the nth node from the end of a linked list in one pass and return the head.

examples.explanation: The 2nd node from the end is 4, which is removed.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 30]
intuition: Use two pointers separated by n nodes so the second pointer lands on the node before the target.

approaches.name: Two-pointer one-pass
approaches.description: Advance the first pointer n+1 times, then move both pointers until first reaches the end.
approaches.why_not: Measuring list length then removing requires two separate passes, but it still works if needed.

interview_qa.a: It handles removal of the head cleanly when n equals list length.

interview_qa.q: How many passes does this use?
interview_qa.a: One pass after the initial pointer gap setup.
